title: edhoc: process_message_1 ignores METHOD from Message 1
description:
File: python/src/lichen/crypto/edhoc.py
Lines: 517-530

EdhocResponder.process_message_1 decodes METHOD_CORR from Message 1 but ignores it:

```python
items = _decode_cbor_sequence(msg1)
# ...
suites_i = items[1]
self._g_x = items[2]
self._c_i = _decode_connection_id(items[3])
```

The method (items[0] // 4) is never extracted or validated against self.method. This means:

1. If initiator sends METHOD=1 (SIGN_STATIC) but responder expects METHOD=0 (SIGN_SIGN), no error is raised
2. The responder proceeds with its own self.method value regardless of what the initiator requested
3. This can cause interoperability failures or security issues (e.g., responder does static-DH when initiator expects signature)

Fix: Extract method from items[0], verify it matches self.method or dynamically adjust authentication based on negotiated method.
